summary: Typosquat of metamask-sdk harvests wallets, SSH/AWS/git credentials, and browser wallet extensions to hardcoded IP
details: Package name '@wagni_bot/metemask-sdk' is a homoglyph typosquat of the popular 'metamask-sdk' (metemask vs metamask). Both scripts.preinstall and scripts.postinstall in package.json invoke `node postinstall.js`, so the payload runs automatically on `npm install`. postinstall.js enumerates the installer's home, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, and /tmp directories for files matching wallet/seed/mnemonic/keystore names (wallet.json, keystore.json, metamask.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt, etc.), reads ~/.ssh private keys (id_rsa, id_ecdsa, id_ed25519, deploy_key, authorized_keys), ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and.env/.env.local/.env.production files, walks Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium 'Local Extension Settings' for the MetaMask extension ID (nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and the Phantom extension ID (bfnaelmomeimhlpmgjnjophhpkkoljpa), and reads Solana id.json and Ethereum keystore directories. The collected contents are POSTed to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777 via http.request. This is the canonical wallet-stealer / credential harvester pattern executing at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
